what_it_does: This README acts as the entry-point handoff instructions for a coding agent, explaining that the bundle contains HTML/CSS/JS prototypes exported from Claude Design and directing the agent to read the primary design file and all of its imports before implementing.
observations: The bundle is described as a handoff from Claude Design (claude.ai/design).
The design medium is HTML/CSS/JS prototypes, not production code.
Agents are told to find the primary design file under conceptv-design-system/project/ and read it top to bottom.
Agents must follow imports into shared components, CSS, and scripts.
Agents are instructed to ask the user to confirm anything ambiguous before implementing.
Agents are explicitly told not to render the files in a browser or take screenshots unless asked.
The goal is to recreate the designs pixel-perfectly in the target technology, matching visual output rather than copying internal structure.
Bundle contents are listed as this README plus the project/ directory.
standouts: Strong upfront directive to clarify scope before coding.
Explicit prohibition against relying on browser screenshots.
Emphasis on reading the entire primary file and tracing every import.
conventions: README serves as the root entry point for the handoff bundle.
Directory structure is described with a simple hierarchical listing.
concerns: Does not identify the specific primary design file by name, only its parent directory, so the agent must infer which file to open first.
notes: A brief handoff README that tells coding agents where to start reading, what to match, and to ask questions before building.
questions: Which file inside conceptv-design-system/project/ is the primary design file?
What target framework or technology stack should the implementation use?
What specific components, tokens, or patterns does the ConceptV Design System define?
Are there accessibility, responsiveness, or theming requirements beyond visual parity?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches what it does: it is a handoff README that instructs agents how to approach the design bundle.
apparent_themes: Source code as the single source of design truth
intention_signals: It is trying to orchestrate a smooth transition from a design prototype to production code by giving the implementing agent a clear reading order, a visual-fidelity goal, and a stop-and-ask rule for ambiguous requirements.
novelty: Explicitly forbids rendering the prototypes or taking screenshots, asserting that the source files alone contain all needed design information.
contribution: Provides the handoff entry point and reading order: it tells an implementer where to start, to trace all imports, to prioritize visual fidelity, and to seek clarification before coding.
summary_for_embedding: This README is the entry-point handoff instructions for a coding agent implementing the ConceptV Design System prototypes exported from Claude Design.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to direct an implementing agent to read the primary design file and its dependencies, match visual output, and resolve ambiguities before coding.
